prompt: Use architecture skill: design service boundaries for a multi-tenant billing platform moving from modular monolith to services. Include synchronous APIs, asynchronous events, and ownership boundaries.
deliverable_expected: Context and container-level architecture with bounded contexts
API/event contract boundaries with ownership notes
Phased migration plan with blast-radius controls
validation_expected: Failure-mode and dependency risk list
Rollback posture per migration phase
Capacity and SLO assumptions made explicit

scoring_notes.route: Prompt explicitly requests architecture-level boundary design.
scoring_notes.quality: High score requires concrete boundaries, sequencing, and tradeoff defense.

prompt: As architecture lead, write an ADR comparing saga orchestration vs 2PC for cross-service order and inventory consistency under partial failures.
deliverable_expected: Option matrix with constraints and assumptions
validation_expected: Consistency, latency, and operability tradeoffs are quantified
Failure handling semantics are explicit
Decision can be audited later

scoring_notes.route: Architecture decision framing is primary intent.
scoring_notes.quality: High score separates business consistency needs from technical mechanism bias.

prompt: Checkout throughput dropped after traffic doubled. Current design has one shared database and lock contention. Propose a target architecture that restores p95 latency while preserving correctness.
deliverable_expected: Target architecture for write-path and read-path separation
Data consistency strategy for critical invariants
validation_expected: Performance bottleneck model before design changes
Correctness risks and mitigation per step
Operational observability requirements

scoring_notes.route: No explicit skill name, but system-shape redesign dominates.
scoring_notes.quality: High score avoids generic scaling tips and specifies architecture-level controls.

prompt: Design a multi-region API architecture with p95 < 120ms and strict data residency (EU/US) while keeping a unified product experience.
validation_expected: Residency compliance boundaries are explicit
RPO/RTO and failover trigger rules are named
Cross-region consistency assumptions are documented

scoring_notes.quality: High score balances residency, latency, and resilience without hand-waving.

prompt: Frontend teams complain about slow product pages, but root causes involve API fan-out, cache invalidation, and search index lag. Propose the cross-layer redesign.
deliverable_expected: Target architecture for cache, index, and request fan-out
validation_expected: Route rationale explains why architecture beats frontend-only treatment
Failure and staleness modes are addressed
Post-change measurement plan is included

scoring_notes.route: Ambiguous UI symptom but dominant cause is backend/system architecture.
scoring_notes.quality: High score includes concrete invalidation and consistency strategy.

prompt: Plan a token-format change that touches API gateway, mobile apps, and downstream services. We need canary rollout, compatibility windows, and rollback safety.
validation_expected: Backward-compatibility window is explicit
Rollback prerequisites and irreversible steps are named
Cross-client contract testing strategy exists

scoring_notes.route: Release mechanics exist, but primary challenge is architecture compatibility design.
scoring_notes.quality: High score ties rollout stages to dependency graph and blast radius.

prompt: Design the trust-boundary architecture for a B2B platform where customer-managed keys, workload identity, and partner webhooks all coexist.
validation_expected: Compromise scenarios and containment stance are included
Control-plane isolation assumptions are explicit
Auditability and rotation paths are stated

scoring_notes.route: Architecture-level security shape, not only vulnerability scanning.
scoring_notes.quality: High score demonstrates layered controls and operational recovery paths.

prompt: Use architecture skill to score migration options from managed queue vendor A to vendor B without downtime. Include dual-write risks and decommission criteria.
deliverable_expected: Option scoring matrix with weighted criteria
validation_expected: Message ordering and idempotency risks are handled
Rollback trigger and data reconciliation plan are explicit
Decision rationale is reproducible

scoring_notes.quality: High score avoids vendor-marketing bias and quantifies migration risk.

prompt: Incidents show repeated timeout storms. Team asks for retry code fixes, but symptoms suggest topology and dependency coupling issues. Define the durable fix path.
deliverable_expected: Implementation ordering for code and platform changes
validation_expected: Root-cause framing distinguishes local code vs system design causes
Reliability SLO and alerting assumptions are explicit
Residual risk after rollout is stated

scoring_notes.route: Prompt mentions code fixes, but durable solution requires architecture intervention.
scoring_notes.quality: High score connects timeout storms to coupling and capacity behavior.

prompt: Create an exit architecture from a proprietary workflow engine so core business processes can run on portable components across cloud providers.
deliverable_expected: Transition plan with coexistence and rollback
validation_expected: Lock-in vectors are enumerated
Data/state migration risks are modeled
Operational ownership after migration is defined

scoring_notes.route: Portability and system decomposition are architecture-first tasks.
scoring_notes.quality: High score includes coexistence strategy, not only target-state diagram.
